collection.Notes: Opening night insanity (i.e. very enthusiastic audience involving a lot of yelling and cheering). The show stopped after technical difficulties through Here For You, and again, Dolly Parton came out to talk to the audience AND sing 9 to 5. There are a few little bloopers and ad-libs. My favorite is when Marc accidentally knocked over the pencils when falling back in his chair, and Megan picked them up. Then when he was supposed to deliberately knock over the pencils, Megan went to pick them up again and said, Just keeps happening, don't it? SJB still sounds a bit sick, but she used it to her advantage and did a crazy fierce Get Out and Stay Out. I was just about on the floor in amazement. A lot of little changes, too. The big one for the master was that they got rid of the dialogue between Andy Karl and Allison Janney in the lunch room scene—now all he does is trip, and then he slinks off.

collection.Notes: Final performance! Filmed in 16:9 widescreen. Few obstructions, only in the wide-shots when there are two heads on either side of the frame, but filmer does his/her best to shoot around them. External audio fed into video from MD recorder, so audio is very clear. A mix of close-ups and wider shots when multiple people were on stage to see their interactions. This was a gut-wrenchingly sad performance. Most of the cast members were in tears at some point or another during the show. In particular, Stephanie was sobbing through I Just Might and Allison started sobbing at the end of One of the Boys. A few minor ad-libs and mishaps. One being when Allison pulled out a tape measure during the urinals bit of One of the Boys. The cast was sad but they were clearly trying to have the times of their lives and go out with a bang. And they fed off the audience energy, which was insane and very supportive.

VOB + Smalls (2.49 GB) - 2nd Generation copy, rare in this condition and was received from SunsetBlvd79’s collection. Much better copy with great colors though it still isn’t as clear as the master copy which isn’t circulating.

collection.Notes:
The stars of the NBC musical drama series Smash reunite virtually for a special one-night-only streaming event: Bombshell in Concert. The event, announced by The Actors Fund in association with PEOPLE, features the never-seen sold-out live performance of Bombshell in Concert that electrified Broadway at the Minskoff Theater in New York City in 2015.
The evening was introduced by two-time Academy Award winner Renée Zellweger, and during intermission, Julie Klausner of “Difficult People” hosted a live, virtual reunion with the show's original cast members, including: Christian Borle, Jaime Cepero, Will Chase, Brian d'Arcy James, Jack Davenport, Ann Harada, Megan Hilty, Jeremy Jordan, Katharine McPhee, Andy Mientus, Debra Messing, Leslie Odom Jr., Krysta Rodriguez and Wesley Taylor.
The People version of the proshot cut the song At Your Feet.

collection.Master Notes: Excellent capture of this wonderful new musical in the final week of previews! Shot with alternate camera in a great mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups; some washout can sometimes be seen on wider shots. Minor obstruction on the bottom that rarely cuts off action, it only obstructs when they lean forward which is almost immediately remedied, so the obstruction is well worked around. Some wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. Overall an excellent capture of Jasmine Amy Rogers’ star turn in this fun show!

This is a near perfect capture with no washout, no obstruction, and just one quick dropout in act one. It’s filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans. Two DVDs.

collection.Cast: Stephanie J. Block (Cher [Star]), Teal Wicks (Cher [Lady]), Micaela Diamond (Cher [Babe]), Jarrod Spector (Sonny Bono), Michael Berresse (Bob Mackie), Michael Campayno (Rob Camilletti), Matthew Hydzik (Gregg Allman), Emily Skinner (Georgia Holt)
collection.Notes: Beautiful HD close-ups but some long drop-outs and quite a few obstructed scenes. The rake in the mezzanine at the Neil Simon kinda stinks and I'm pretty sure the ushers were playing flashlight tag with each other the entire show. but I began to care a lot less as the night went on Video is very Cher-centric...choreography in Dark Lady is captured beautifully, but for the rest of the show I was mostly focused on getting those Stephanie, Teal, and Micaela zooms. We start at the scene before Half Breed and when Stephanie tells everyone to stand up for the finale it's all over for my filming ability.

collection.Cast: Cynthia Erivo (Celie), Jennifer Hudson (Shug Avery), Danielle Brooks (Sofia), Isaiah Johnson (Mister), Kyle Scatliffe (Harpo), Joaquina Kalukango (Nettie)
collection.Notes: Incredible performances all around, but the stand out is an absolutely electric performance by Cynthia. She gives a Tony-worthy performance, and there is a standing ovation for her after I'm Here as well as at the end of the show. A nice capture overall. There is a one minute blackout during Huckleberry Pie and a two minute blackout during Our Prayer; the rest of the show is fully captured with just a couple quick dropouts. There is one head that occasionally blocks the front left section of the stage, but it's worked around whenever possible. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans. 2 discs.

collection.Notes: Filmed in 4K (released as 1080p and 4K). External audio source. A little spotlight washout in a few scenes, but camera settings were adjusted from previous video, so overall much improved (no more rolling bars). A little shaky now and then, some obstruction from heads in a couple scenes—shot around as well as possible. Jennifer Simard downright SHINES as Joanne and does perhaps the best Ladies Who Lunch I've heard—too bad she isn't eligible for a Tony for this role. Never to be sold except by master, never to be posted anywhere online.

collection.Notes:
Technical Notes: Excellent camcorder video captured in 4K. No obstructions and no washout. A brief dropout around the beginning of Have I Got A Guy For You. Great audio. Shot from the back of the balcony centre stage. [A]

Performance Notes: A just-about-perfect capture of a hugely significant evening for Marianne Elliott’s Company: the first performance following the Tony Award nomination ceremony where the production became the most nominated revival of the season. Matt Doyle's debut Tony nomination, EIGHT-time Tony-nominee Patti LuPone, Katina Lenk taking on her deafening snub with one of the best performances of this character I've ever seen, AND MOST IMPORTANTLY, it is the very performance that gave us Chris Harper pays my salary. (The video features a modified pre-show and post-show speech from Rashidra Scott, acknowledging the talkback following the performance.) When people say bootlegs are the preservation of theatrical history, this is what they're fuckin talking about.

collection.Cast: Katrina Lenk (Bobbie), Patti LuPone (Joanne), Nicholas Rodriguez (u/s Larry), Matt Doyle (Jamie), Etai Benson (Paul), Jennifer Simard (Sarah), Christopher Sieber (Harry), Rashidra Scott (Susan), Greg Hildreth (Peter), Nikki Renée Daniels (Jenny), Christopher Fitzgerald (David), Claybourne Elder (Andy), Bobby Conte Thornton (PJ), Manu Narayan (Theo), Anisha Nagarajan (New Yorker), Britney Coleman (New Yorker), Kathryn Allison (New Yorker), Matt Wall (s/w New Yorker), Javier Ignacio (New Yorker), Heath Saunders (New Yorker)
collection.Notes:
Technical Notes: Stageshot. Excellent capture. No obstructions. Almost no washout. No dropouts. Excellent audio (though is apologetically bombarded by frequent scream laughing from me.) Theres a brief show stop immediately after the title number, and the video captures the curtain coming down and the resuming of the performance. The first act unfortunately loses its picture a few minutes after Not Getting Married today - especially unfortunate as the performance was one of the last which featured rain during Marry Me a Little. Shot from front row stage left. [A]
Performance Notes: This production is the most premium musical theatre experience I have ever had in my life. I could simply go on for hours about how magnificent this entire production was - Katrinas bottomlessly fleshed-out interpretation of Bobbie, Patti LuPones masterclass display of performing, Jennifer Simards nomination-worthy comedy, Christopher Siebers perfect matching and prodding of his scene partner, Claybourne Elders show-stealing acting, Bobby Contes booming vocals, Marianne Elliots ability to make this total re-envisioning utterly airtight dramaturgically, Bunny Christies masterful and detailed set design, Neil Austins exacting lighting design executed to meticulous precision, Ian Dickinsons complex sound design. This is a thorough gift to Broadway and this video captures a performance at its absolutely perfect settling point.

collection.Master Notes: Great 4k capture of this incredible show.  Shot with a mix of mediums, wides, and close-ups.  Shot around a railing which sometimes appears in shot and can cause wide shots to be tilted slightly more than usual, and there are moments of wandering/focusing on the wrong thing, particularly during the first ten minutes or so.  Includes bows.  Other than that, this is a great capture of this hilarious new musical that I had absolutely the best time at.

collection.Master Notes:
Filmed in 4K from orchestra. Rare obstructed moments when action was on the right side and person in front leaned into the aisle. Dropouts for applause and a few moments of late-comers arriving. A few moments of camera going out of focus. Otherwise an excellent capture of Dee Roscioli's debut as Madeline. Includes curtain call, special recognition for Dee's debut, and Simard's BC/EFA speech. 
NFT (NOT FOR TRADE) and sales only through master until December 28, 2025.

collection.Master Notes: Masters first complete video of this show. The first 2 minutes of Act 1 and the first 5 minutes of Act 2 are audio only due to usher activity. Other than that, there are only a couple short dropouts. The start of Act 2 is wide blindshot but gets better after a few minutes. Act 1 has an annoying head that covers some of stage left (somebody sat right in front of me roughly one minute into the show) but the head is gone in Act 2. Some readjustment, washout, and pixelation throughout. A good mix of mediums and closeups with a few wideshots. Shot from Mezz Left.

collection.Master Notes: Great close up stage shot of Natalie's second (unexpected) performance as Helen. Some moments of shakiness and readjusting and a couple moments of obstructions for latecomers. The video can be a tad bit dark, but nothing is missed because of it. The transformation scene has been totally redone with projections. Sadly, the projections cannot be seen in the video despite being able to see them live. Overall, great capture of the locked (and much improved!) version of the show!

collection.Master Notes:
Filmed in 4K from orchestra. Excellent capture of one of the final preview performances before opening. Minor obstruction when the person in front of me leaned into the aisle or lifted his arm. Occasional moments where camera goes out of focus. Dropouts for applause and a couple moments when late-comers arrived. One scene in Act 2 where the framing was a tad off (but all action can still be seen). Loud and responsive audience, especially laughter - super fun! Includes partially obstructed curtain call. 
NFT (NOT FOR TRADE) and sales only through master until August 28, 2025.

Bows not included, but the dance party on stage after the music is included. Closing night and the energy of cast and audience is completely electric. Includes Audio (tracked and untracked), digital program, and video. Never to be sold. Gifting Encouraged.

collection.Master Notes: Great 4k capture of Jordan’s, Maia’s, Phillip’s, and Shea’s final show.  About the first two and a half minutes are just dark between everyone’s entrance onstage and the actual start of show, and much of the opening number is completely obstructed due to usher activity.  Shot around/above one very annoying head (with spiky hair no less), with frequent readjusting during most of Act 1 to work around it, with varying success, and the video has one brief dropout during The Chips Are Down, after which visibility much improves.  Lots of closeups on Jordan, Maia, and Phillip whenever possible.  Extremely emotional show, with multiple midshow standing ovations.  Sorry for all the whooing and the violent sobbing near the end (I’m not sorry).  Includes highly obstructed footage of We Raise Our Cups and Lillias’s speeches for the departing cast members.

Shot in a great mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. Some increased wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. Audio is patched in from an external source. Does not include Bows or We Raise Our Cups. Overall a wonderful capture that only gets messy when navigating head obstruction.

Manuel (s/w Worker)
collection.Master Notes: Great 4k capture of this touring cast!  Filmed with a mix of mostly wides and mediums with a few close-ups.  After a slightly lengthy interval, it was announced that Randy Cain would be taking over as Hades and Darius Manuel would be swinging on as a worker.  Some slight graininess to the picture at times and the very far left of the stage is somewhat obstructed by the lighting rig, including the passage that acts as the entrance/exit to Hadestown.  Occasional wandering/readjusting throughout, but little obstruction except when the person in front of me fidgeted with their hair.  Slight amount of camera fog at the beginning, and there’s an extended dropout during Anyway the Wind Blows due to latecomers in my row.  General warning for intense strobing lights, timestamp: 1:22:58-1:23:10.  Sorry for crying like all throughout Road to Hell (Reprise) but Jaylon Hermes…they really got to me.  Includes We Raise Our Cups.

Allison is in frame whenever possible, with a slight focus on her at times - I was a bit of a lesbian mess this performance and it shows.  She truly gave it her everything, and her Persephone will always be so special to me.  Heads sometimes show up on the bottom of the picture but don’t obstruct anything.  General warning for intense strobing lights, timestamp: 1:25:16-1:25:28.  Includes (a deeply emotional) We Raise Our Cups and Lillias’s goodbye speech.

collection.Notes:
Previews! Picks up after Road to Hell. Decent capture with a lot of beautiful zooms, two really annoying heads, and a drop out occasionally but never for long. (For the first time in my mastering career I kept putting the camera down after musical numbers because I couldn't NOT clap). This was my first time seeing the show and hearing this music so I was sometimes focused on the wrong things. Oh well. Overall, I love this video. I got home at 2AM after filming it and just SOBBED while I rewatched some of my favorite moments. WHAT A SHOW!

Absolutely in love with this new cast. Amazing performances from all, but my favorite is definitely Phillip Boykin. He brings complete new life to Hades. Includes video, audio (tracked by Medium Observation and untracked), and cast board photo.

Some wandering and unfocusing throughout. Fairly Patrick Page centered, as all of Epic 1 and Epic 3 are focused on him as well as some other typically-Orpheus centric moments. Blackout during the beginning of Lady of the Underground, and brief blackout during Epic 3. Includes curtain call, We Raise Our Cups, and speeches by Reeve, Patrick, and director Rachel Chavkin. collection.Cast: Reeve Carney (Orpheus), Eva Noblezada (Eurydice), Amber Gray (Persephone), Patrick Page (Hades), André De Shields (Hermes), Tara Jackson (u/s Fate), Jewelle Blackman (Fate), Kay Trinidad (Fate), Emily Afton (Worker), Malcolm Armwood (Worker), Timothy Hughes (Worker), Grace Yoo (Worker), Trent Saunders (Worker)
collection.Notes:
Amber Gray's final performance in Hadestown. Full show is filmed, including the standing ovation that Amber got before the performance even started, right up to the band exiting. Moderate obstruction from the box/bar, etc. Dropouts and shakiness during applause breaks, especially during all of the standing ovations that happened for Amber—what can I say? It was such an emotional night and I was more focused on being in the moment than getting a 4K video. However, what is captured is high quality, and is of a very memorable show. For transparency, some screenshots include the obstructed/less-than-HQ moments, so people know what they're purchasing/watching.

collection.Notes: An absolutely stellar tour cast with fantastic performances all around. Some great nuanced interpretations of the characters make the show fresh. This capture is nearly perfect with just one dropout toward the end of My Shot that lasts less than a minute. There is one head that occasionally blocks the actors' legs on the right side of the stage. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

There are no major blackouts, just a few quick dropouts, mostly between scenes. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. collection.Notes:
Pro-shot filmed in May 2022 with the Off-West End cast, and released on September 16, 2022. Originally released on Roku. Veronica Sawyer does her best to survive her senior year while navigating the beautiful but cruel Heathers, the new to school Jason J.D. Dean, and the constant pressure to fit in with everyone else.
Three versions exist, the one released on Roku, the one released in UK cinemas without ad breaks or BTS moments, and one that contains a BTS of the filming process with no ad breaks.
Multiple Subtitle files exist to correspond to the timing of the different versions.

collection.Master Notes:
Filmed in 4K, released in 1080p and 4K. Filmed between a couple heads that occasionally got in the way when they leaned toward each other or when the guy lifted his arm to scratch his head - but rarely blocks significant action. A couple drop-outs when people nearby came in late or left early. Camera did not do well with one scene using a lot of blue light, but the rest of the show is beautifully filmed. 
Never to be sold except by master, never to be posted anywhere online. NFT (not for trade) and sales only through master until November 28, 2023.

collection.Notes: A great capture of the American premiere at La Jolla Playhouse. The incredibly talented cast delivers the score beautifully. Michael and Ciara both give powerhouse performances. A really fantastic capture with no obstruction, no washout, and just two quick dropouts in Act One. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans. A

Mix of wide, medium, and closer shots for group or individual focus depending on the scene. Includes curtain call. Seat neighbor was sometimes VERY loud with her cheering—just beware.

collection.Notes: Cole Porter, and Sam and Bella Spewack’s smash hit musical loosely based on Shakespeare’s The Taming of the Shrew. The 2024 London revival directed by Bartlett Sher and starring Stephanie J. Block and Adrian Dunbar was filmed live during its run at the Barbican and released in cinemas in the U.S., U.K., Ireland, Australia, and selected territories in November 2024. Aired on PBS May 30, 2025.

collection.Notes: Pretty decent wide-shot phone boot taken from the left Mezz. Safety bar can be seen at the bottom through the show, but only covers up action in 2 or 3 songs. Some unfocusing throughout when the reflection of the orchestra boxes can be seen. Mariand Torres' debut as Tamara and the cast and audience absolutely LOVED her. Includes composer Matt Gould's speech introducing her. Very enthusiastic crowd. Such a beautiful show with an incredible, catchy score. Includes video, audio (tracked and untracked), and cast board photo.

collection.Cast: Eden Espinosa (Tamara de Lempicka), Amber Iman (Rafaela), Andrew Samonsky (Tadeusz Lempicki), George Abud (Marinetti), Natalie Joy Johnson (Suzy Solidor), Nathaniel Stampley (The Baron), Beth Leavel (The Baroness), Zoe Glick (Kizette), Alex Aquilino (Ensemble), Lauren Blackman (Ensemble), Stephen Brower (Ensemble), Kyle Brown (Ensemble), Holli' Conway (Ensemble), Abby Matsusaka (Ensemble), Jimin Moon (Ensemble), Khori Michelle Petinaud (Ensemble), Ximone Rose (Ensemble), Nicholas Ward (Ensemble)
collection.Notes: Very good wide-shot phone boot filmed from the right orchestra. Minimal washout except a few scenes that are super bright. Less than 3% of the show. No obstruction except heads on the very bottom of the stage, only covers shoes in certain scenes. Slight unfocusing throughout in high-contrast scenes. Delightful mess-up when Eden drops one of the cookies during the Madeleine scene. Exceptional audience that actually appreciates this beautiful art that has become my favorite show this year. So sad it didn't last too long on Broadway, because it truly is a gem of a show.

collection.Master Notes: Solid capture of the new cast of this stunning production! Shot in a great mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups; more wide than usual due to the nature of the staging. Some increased wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. Audio is patched in from an external source. Two major dropouts due to usher activity; 7 minutes total before/during/after “Feed Me” and the last minute of the show are audio only due to usher activity. Obstruction on the left side and across the bottom that only occasionally blocks action. Overall a solid capture that’s only marred by some obstruction and dropouts.

Excellent performance from Nicholas Christopher. Includes video, audio (tracked and untracked), and cast board photo.

collection.Cast: Caissie Levy (Diana), Jamie Parker (Dan), Jack Wolfe (Gabe), Eleanor Worthington-Cox (Natalie), Jack Ofrecio (Henry), Trevor Dion Nicholas (Dr. Fine/Dr. Madden)
collection.Master Notes: Fantastic capture of this incredible West End transfer. Shot in a beautiful mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. More wides than usual to catch the simply amazing staging and blocking. Some increased wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout, as well as my backpack occasionally blocking the very bottom of the stage, but nothing is missed. The overhang cuts off the very top of the set and occasionally obscures faces when they're far back in the top rooms. Some non-disruptive rustling heard throughout. Truly an excellent capture of this excellent show.

collection.Cast: Caissie Levy (Diana), Jamie Parker (Dan), Jack Wolfe (Gabe), Eleanor Worthington-Cox (Natalie), Jack Ofrecio (Henry), Trevor Dion Nicholas (Dr. Fine/Dr. Madden)
collection.Notes:
Filmed at Wyndham's Theatre from September 9-11 2024 during the show's West End transfer from its original run at the Donmar Warehouse. 

Multiple versions circulate:
- Censored audio (Aired on PBS's Great Performances May 9, 2025)
- Uncensored audio with hardcoded timestamp in top-left corner and different scene changes, leaked theatrical release version
- Full uncensored version ripped from Amazon. Swear words are included, and the camera doesn't cut away during them. No time code present.

Check with traders.

collection.Notes:
T/W: This video contains a PETA advertisement at the beginning, which contains graphic images and may be distressing.  This was included by the master on release.

First performance of the pre-Broadway run. A rare capture due to the fact the show ran 2 hours and 34 minutes, so cuts are nearly inevitable. Includes three already cut songs, I Could Get Used to This, The Line-Up Reprise #2, and Gonna Build a Wall. Beautiful capture with no obstructions. A very fun show, a top-notch cast, and memorable songs, sure to play many years on Broadway. A+

collection.Notes:
This 1080p HD proshot with soundboard audio was released to Netflix in 2013, but was filmed in mid-December of 2009, a few months before the production closed. File information varies across traders.
Leah Greenhaus and Marissa O'Donnell performed as Young Fiona and Teen Fiona, respectively, in I Know It's Today. Rozi Baker performed as Young Fiona in Big Bright Beautiful World and I'm a Believer, while Tessa Albertson performed as Teen Fiona in I'm a Believer. Swings and previous cast members were included as Duloc Dolls in the ensemble for What's Up, Duloc.

collection.Notes: Final performance of the pre-Broadway run, with many changes. Nearly 25 minutes of cuts and changes since the first performance, including new songs. collection.Master Notes: Great 4K capture of a rare set of understudies, including Sydney’s debut as Betty! Some head / railing obstruction at the front of the stage that occasionally blocks off characters sitting / laying down. More messily filmed and wideshot than usual due to usher activity, nearly all action is still captured. Increased moments of wandering / readjustment and unfocusing. Gets better in Act Two. Includes curtain call, the post-show BC/EFA speech, and credits, audio is fed from external source.

collection.Master Notes:
Filmed in 4K from mezzanine. Excellent capture of Rashidra Scott as Norma - steady filming throughout, almost no obstructions except for a head that is sometimes seen on the lowest part of the screen but does not obstruct any action. A couple of brief dropouts aside from applause. Only a couple of moments where camera goes out of focus. Very enthusiastic audience to support Rashidra who was utterly outstanding. Includes curtain call and David's BC/EFA speech that were recorded on another device, which is a little grainy. 
NFT (NOT FOR TRADE) and sales only through master until January 28, 2026.

collection.Master Notes: Excellent 4K capture of this anticipated revival’s first preview! Some head / railing obstruction at the front of the stage that occasionally blocks off characters sitting / laying down. 30 second blackout in the New Year Eve scene. There was a glitch with the camera that led to some zooming out without input, which sometimes affects the video (primarily in the first ten minutes). Increased moments of wandering / readjustment and unfocusing throughout. One scene is very dark, therefore very difficult to see and takes a little bit to focus on the main action. Includes curtain call, audio is fed from external source.

Hamilton (Lisa), Brandon Mel Borkowsky (John), Jimin Moon (Hog Eye/Morino), Sydney Jones (Dorothy), Olivia Lacie Andrews (Nancy), Shayna McPherson (Katherine/Camera Operator)
collection.Master Notes: Excellent 4k capture of Mandy taking on the lead role in this revival and giving a powerhouse performance.  Shot with a mix of mediums, wides, and closeups.  Occasional minor obstruction from a railing and some washout during wideshots.  Unfortunately, you can often hear the people behind me whispering and someone chose to blow their nose very loudly at the most inopportune moments.  Captures the filmed aspect of the production whenever possible.  General warning for intense isolated flashing lights: timestamp (1:58:05-1:58:30) and that the filmed footage can appear to flicker.  Lots of wandering during the very dark scene near the end, sorry.  Overall a beautiful capture of Mandy’s beautifully unique take on Norma.  Includes bows/credits.

collection.Master Notes: Near-perfect wide-shot phone boot from center orchestra. Minor obstruction by heads in the very front left that only covers 2 or 3 lines. Funny improv about Rose recreating Frankenstein. Nathan messes up the timing in the Ruth breakdown scene, leading to a long time of him wandering the stage being confused.

collection.Cast: Dee Roscioli (Céline Dion), Max Jenkins (Jack), Cayleigh Capaldi (Rose), Joel Waggoner (Ruth), Tommy Bracco (Victor Garber/Luigi), Marcus Antonio (The Seaman/Iceberg), Lisa Howard (Molly Brown), Brandon Contreras (Cal), Terrence Williams Jr. (Background Vocalist), Rae Davenport (Background Vocalist), Sara Gallo (Background Vocalist)
collection.Master Notes:
Decent wide-shot phone video from the center. In scenes where Brandon was across from someone else and I couldn't capture both, I deferred to Brandon, since it was their last show. Improv is a magic battle between Jack and Rose. Joel added a bunch of really funny stuff as Ruth. Brandon breaks when he sees a banner at the top of the theatre spelled by the sound engineers saying F-A-G.

Includes BCEFA speech and a Goodbye to Brandon speech from Dee. Very emotional audience. So happy to see Brandon get all the love they deserve.

collection.Cast: Dee Roscioli (Céline Dion), Michael Evan Williams (Jack), Chani Maisonet (u/s Rose), Michael Di Liberto (Ruth), Elliott Mattox (Victor Garber/Luigi), Marcus Antonio (The Seaman), Tess Marshall (Molly Brown), Brad Greer (t/r Cal), Sara Gallo (Background Vocalist), Terrence Williams Jr. (Background Vocalist)
collection.Notes: Very good wide-shot phone boot from center orchestra. Unfortunately, some action on the very far right side gets missed, I'd say less than 5% of the show. Good video to see some understudies and temp replacements. Michael D's comedy as Ruth is HYSTERICAL. The ranting scene is especially funny. Also the scene after All By Myself where he makes some fun Pride jokes. Improv is Jack and Rose doing a thumb war. Cast has excellent vocals as always. Brad's songs as Cal are absolutely divine. Lively audience. Includes video, audio (tracked and untracked), and cast board photo.

collection.Cast: Dee Roscioli (Céline Dion), Michael Evan Williams (Jack), Lindsay Heather Pearce (Rose), Russell Daniels (Ruth), Elliott Mattox (Victor Garber/Luigi), Marcus Antonio (The Seaman/Iceberg), Anne Fraser Thomas (Molly Brown), Brandon Contreras (Cal), Sara Gallo (Background Vocalist), Chani Maisonet (Background Vocalist), Garrett Poladian (Background Vocalist)
collection.Notes: Near flawless wide-shot phone video from the front middle. All action captured well except scenes that were completely offstage in the aisle (less than 2% of the show). Hilarious physical comedy in the improv which has Lindsay and Michael acting like Godzilla Vs. Kong. The faces and poses made are meme-able for sure. Awesome vocals as usual from everyone on the ship. Still my favorite show. Includes video, audio (tracked and untracked), and cast board photo.

collection.Cast: Dee Roscioli (Céline Dion), Michael Evan Williams (Jack), Cayleigh Capaldi (Rose), Joel Waggoner (Ruth), Tommy Bracco (Victor Garber/Luigi), Marcus Antonio (The Seaman/Iceberg), Lisa Howard (Molly Brown), Terrence Williams Jr. (t/r Cal), Barnaby Reiter (Background Vocalist), Tess Marshall (Background Vocalist)
collection.Master Notes: Decent wide-shot phone video from the center. In scenes where Michael was across from someone else and I couldn't capture both, I deferred to Michael, since it was his last show. Improv is a parody of Brokeback Mountain. Humor Highlight when Dee adds crotch hair to her costume and scratches it. Includes BCEFA speech and a Goodbye to Michael speech from Dee. Very emotive audience. So happy to see Michael get all the love he deserves.  Includes video, audio (tracked and untracked), and cast board photo. Never to be sold. Gifting Encouraged.

collection.Cast: Tess Marshall (u/s Céline Dion), Michael Evan Williams (Jack), Cayleigh Capaldi (Rose), Joel Waggoner (Ruth), Tommy Bracco (Victor Garber/Luigi), Marcus Antonio (The Seaman/Iceberg), Lisa Howard (Molly Brown), Terrence Williams Jr. (t/r Cal), Sara Gallo (Background Vocalist), Barnaby Reiter (Background Vocalist), Billie Aken-Tyers (Background Vocalist)
collection.Master Notes: Almost perfect wide-shot capture. Possibly my best video of all time so far. Only moments not captured are when the cast is in the audience or behind me. Tess has really grown into her Céline role. Vocals are 10x better than my March video. Improv includes a Hamilton Rap. Joel makes Cayleigh break down when he calls her Theme Park Elsa multiple times. Excellent performances by all, as usual. Includes BCEFA speech. Never to be sold. Gifting Encouraged. Includes video, audio (tracked and untracked), and cast board photo.

collection.Master Notes: Fantastic performances from the entire original cast. Jessie's voice fits beautifully with the score, and she gives a gut-wrenching performance of She Used to Be Mine. Drew, Kimiko, Keala, and Christopher are each hilarious and endearing. Nicely captured with just a few quick dropouts and no major blackouts. There is one head on the left that can occasionally be seen, but it never blocks the action. There is some slight washout in some of the wider shots. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

collection.Master Notes: A nearly perfect video and ABSOLUTELY PERFECT performance. Small obstructions occur at Joe's table (but who cares) and during Negative. The rest of show, however, is captured gorgeously (and this time not diagonally). The video is very Shoshana focused, but I try to give everyone (yes, Jeremy too) their moments. The colors are absolutely vivid and I was trying new stabilization settings on my camera and god I'm gonna keep it this way. Do not list this video with an exact date.

collection.Cast: Sara Bareilles (Jenna), Anastacia McCleskey (u/s Becky), Lenne Klingaman (Dawn), Gavin Creel (Dr. Pomatter), Christopher Fitzgerald (Ogie), Benny Elledge (Cal), Larry Marshall (Joe), Ben Thompson (Earl), Tiffany Denise Hobbs (u/s Nurse Norma), Stephanie Torns (Francine Pomatter), Riley Summer Perler (Lulu), Molly Hager (Mother)

Sometimes we're on a slant and sometimes things aren't centered. Deal with it. I did the best I could while feeling comfortable and it's HD.

collection.Cast: Jeremy Jordan (Tony), Solea Pfeiffer (Maria), Matthew James Thomas (Riff), Karen Olivo (Anita), George Akram (Bernardo), Drew Wildman Foster (Action), Jose Moreno Brooks (Chino), Kyle Selig (Baby John), Anthony Chatmon II (A-Rab), Jeff Smith (Diesel), Mike Schwitter (Big Deal), Kevin Chamberlin (Officer Krupke), Jennifer Sánchez (Rosalia), Erica Dorfler (Consuelo)
collection.Notes: This concert version of the show is abridged with shortened scenes, simple costumes, and no sets or choreography, but Jeremy, Solea, and Karen are absolutely phenomenal with soaring vocals and emotional performances. Very well captured with no dropouts, no obstruction, and no washout. The stage is filmed directly most of the time; the screens are occasionally filmed, usually when the actors are not onstage. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

collection.Notes: Lisa, Annaleigh & Kris' first performance in the company. Annaleigh sings the optional high note in No One Mourns the Wicked, and I think this is the only time this was captured (especially because she almost never did that). Overall AMAZING performance and chemistry. The acting and singing could not have been any better. Lisa was in top-notch form. Annaleigh was beyond quixotic and the rest of the cast was just on fire. A+

collection.Notes: Final performance for Stephanie J. Block. Great capture of possibly the best performance ever given by an Elphaba. SJB lit the stage on fire, honestly this is one of the best individual performances I have ever attended. With such power and control, this is one Wicked NOT to be missed, you will be amazed! Kendra and SJB get emotional during For Good and it's a beautiful moment between them.

Megan Hilty's last show in the Broadway run (she will reprise the role of Glinda in the Los Angeles run).
A very Megan-centric recording, good close-ups and follows the action very well. Includes detailed scene selection, playbill scans at the start (DVD 1) and stage door recordings (DVD 2). One of, if not the best performance of Megan.

collection.Notes: Shoshana, David, Rue and Michelle's last performance. The whole cast was really having fun, very high audience energy throughout. Megan and Shoshana have a really funny bit during Popular, and the crowd went wild. Camera wanders quite a bit and is shaky at points due to the blind filming, plus some spotlight washout. Still, this recording is an absolute gem. Includes Playbill scans and Megan's post-show speech.

collection.Notes: First five minutes of each act are shaky with cover-ups (I've never seen so many latecomers at a show), and there are also blackouts after some songs in Act One. Incredible close-ups; you can see tears in Eden's eyes during her heart-wrenching “I'm Not that Girl.” Audience is electric, a satisfying show overall. Gaelen, in her early 20s, brings an interesting take to Morrible's role, as probably the youngest actress to play her.

collection.Notes: Great 4K capture of this cast! Multiple brief blackouts during the first half hour or so as ushers walked past, and a few scattered during Act Two (totalling about two and a half minutes). There is some flickering throughout the video that isn’t too distracting but might take away from some scenes. Includes curtain call, audio is fed from external source.

collection.Notes: Wicked in Concert was a television special featuring re-imagined musical arrangements created for the event, performed by celebrity artists from the worlds of film, pop, music, and television. At its core, it was a musical celebration of the iconic Broadway musical's score written by composer Stephen Schwartz. Directed by Baayork Lee, the special aired on PBS on August 29, 2021, and was hosted by Kristin Chenoweth and Idina Menzel.

Soto (Witch's Mother), Peter Ermides (Witch's Father/Ozian Official), Alicia Albright (s/w Ensemble), Jenny Florkowski (s/w Ensemble), Alison Jantzie (s/w Ensemble), Dashi Mitchell (s/w Ensemble), Nicky Venditti (s/w Ensemble)
collection.Notes: Filmed in 4K (released as 1080p and 4K). A historic night on Broadway, with Brittney Johnson's first show as full-time Glinda after having been a standby for 3 years, being the first POC to play the role ever. Also Sharon Sach's first show as Madame Morrible after a transfer from the Tour, making her Broadway debut. No significant obstructions, minimal spotlight washout—excellent picture throughout. Only significant dropout is at the start of “Dancing Through Life” (literally only 20 seconds). Never to be sold except by master, never to be posted anywhere online.

Stage shot with no zooms, and often is unfocused, but audio is decent.

collection.Notes:
Highlights from three different shows (October 30, 2005 / January 8, 2006 / May 28, 2006), released in one DVD by the master.
October 30, 2005 - Wickeds second anniversary. Brandi Chavonne Massey went on for Shoshana on this day. Highlights include some short clips of the Wizards Chamber and part of Defying Gravity. Also includes a clip from Wicked Day 2005 with Megan Hilty and Rihanna.
January 8, 2006 - Last show for Shoshana Bean, David Ayers, Rue McClanahan, and Michelle Federer. Highlights include: part of Defying Gravity, part of As Long As Youre Mine, the catfight, No Good Deed, part of March of the Witch Hunters, For Good, the Finale, and the goodbye speech from Megan Hilty.
May 28, 2006 - Megan Hiltys last show with the Broadway company. Highlights include part of What is This Feeling, parts of Dancing Through Life, the train station scene, part of Defying Gravity (the bit with the speech, no flying scene vid), parts of Thank Goodness, Wicked Witch of the East, parts of Wonderful, parts of As Long As Youre Mine, the end of March of the Witch Hunters, Kiamo Ko and For Good.

collection.Notes:
The most common fileset (MP3 tracked - 195 MB) that usually circulates as this recording is incorrect, as it actually features Jennifer Laura Thompson, and seems to be dated November 28, 2004. The correct audio with Megan Hilty is MP3 tracked - 291 MB and ends after For Good. Slightly quiet, not as bad on the overload as many early Wicked audios.
This audio does frequently skip a few seconds, more so in Act 2 than Act 1. In Act 1, this happens at the following points: twice in the Room Assignments scene (first from Glinda's “You can all come visit me!” to Elphaba's “Madame Morrible, we have not yet received our room assignments”, then from Elphaba's “But I promised Father!” to “Something just comes over me sometimes”), in The Wizard and I (from “help me meet the Wizard” to “meet the Wizard, once I prove my worth”), in What Is This Feeling (from “my roommate is” to “Does it have a name?”), in the scene before Something Bad (from Elphaba's “Animals should be seen and not heard?!” to Dillamond's “if only it were just a matter of words on a chalkboard”), in the transition after Something Bad (skips to Fiyero's “I'm sure I won't last longer at this school than any of the others”), in Defying Gravity (from “But 'til I try, I'll never know” to the guard's “In the name of His Supreme Ozness!”).
Act 2 starts a bit into No One Mourns the Wicked (Reprise) (from “where'er she goes”), and skips at the following points: in Thank Goodness (from “share our ending vicariously” to the first “what happens when all your dreams come true”), in The Wicked Witch of the East (from “just the girl in the mirror” to Nessarose's “Elphaba, please don't leave me!”), in Wonderful (from “dime-a-dozen mediocrities” to “got carried away”), twice in the Catfight scene (first from Glinda's “to have a house fall on you” to “maybe not an accident”, then from Glinda's “taking things that don't belong to us” to “Yes, I do” after the slap), seven times in No Good Deed (first from the start of Elphaba's chanting to “flesh not be torn”, then from “Let him never die” to “I don't even know what I'm reading”, then from “Already dead or bleeding?” to the first “No good deed goes unpunished”, then from “That's my new creed” to “Nessa...”, then from the second “Fiyero!” to “seeking good”, then from the fourth “No good deed goes unpunished” to “what well-meant did”, then from the final “No good-” to the middle of the last note), seven times in March of the Witch Hunters (jumping straight to the first “kill her”, then skipping the word “and” in the second “find her and kill her”, and then going from “Evil effectively-” to Boq's “With the Witch!”, from Boq's “I'm not the only one” to “grievance to repay”, then skipping Morrible's “Yes, I suppose it was just her time” and “And now you're getting what you want!”, and finally going from “I would join you if I could” to “But good!”), five times in the Kiamo Ko scene (from Dorothy's crying to Elphaba's “see your Aunt Em”, then from Elphaba mocking Dorothy's crying to “Chistery, there you are”, skipping Glinda's “I know you don’t want to hear this but somebody’s got to say it: You are out of control! I mean, come on, they're just shoes, let it go!”, then going from Glinda's “It's Fiyero, isn't it?” to Elphaba's “I surrender”, then from Glinda's “I don't care!” to “I don't understand”), and in For Good (from Elphaba's “Just look at me” to “You can do all I couldn't do”).

Multiple copies circulate:
VOB + Smalls (3.65 GB) - 1st generation from the master. 1 Disc. Unfortunately, the actual tape transfer is rather bad and results in a lot of washout. Includes extras: NY1 Review Segment, Today Show performance and Letterman performance.
VOB + Smalls (4.80 GB) & (7.46 GB) - 2 Disc copy. Noted as the better version of this video. Unfortunately, the color is semi off and the picture is rather soft.
VOB + Smalls (2.38 GB) - 1st generation master tape re-transferred by NYCG8R in 2011. 1 Disc. Amazingly clearer picture and more vibrant colors. Includes an outside shot of a Wicked poster before the show starts. Screenshots below are from this version.
collection.Release Format: {2011 Master Tape Re-Transfer} - VOB + Smalls (2.38 GB)

collection.Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Kristin Chenoweth (Glinda), Taye Diggs (t/r Fiyero), Joel Grey (The Wizard), Carole Shelley (Madame Morrible), Michelle Federer (Nessarose), Christopher Fitzgerald (Boq), William Youmans (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Master Notes:
Last three caps are from the original DV capture shot in PAL. It's a 21 year old video. If it were a child, I'd be buying it a drink now, so... the quality was great for its time, and it's an improvement over what's floating around.

This was the first full length video I'd filmed, so there was plenty of upper body cramping, which is the reason for the camera shaking, but I thin k it was the only video that came out with Idina/Taye, so we take what we can :-)
collection.Notes:
Restored to the original PAL, though not without a hit from the gen loss incurred along the way. Audio has been filtered to remove the camera noise and excess hiss from the theatre. Image is on the shaky side, and the camera has a hard time locking into focus, combined with being out of sync with the US lights leading to a constant flicker. A pity because its a strong performance by the original cast during the show's ascent.
Sometimes mislabeled as September 1, 2004; Taye was only in the show from December 22 2003 - January 18 2004 while Norbert Leo Butz was out due to a back injury. The audio circulating as January 8, 2004 is ripped from this video, but the audio rip is better quality than the audio in the video.
As of 2025, the master has confirmed that they have the original unconverted recording, which is better quality than the circulating version(s).

collection.Notes:
Incredible performances by the two leading ladies, especially Stephanie who is a total knockout. The video is shot from the balcony, with no obstructions or blackouts but there is a bit of spotlight washout.

There are 3 different versions/transfers of this video being traded in varying quality and resolution. 
The smallest file size version is a significantly lower quality transfer. 
The two larger versions are both much clearer and comparable in quality. The largest file size version has a blue line down the right side of the video, while the middle file size version does not and is significantly rarer.

collection.Notes: This is a vivid 4K video with almost no obstructions. Pretty much everything is centered, with beautiful zooms but also wider shots that showcase the dancing and set design. At times the video appears on an angle because my wrists are weak, but you don't miss anything because of it. There is also washout at times, but I do believe my camera handles the spotlights better than in a lot of Wicked videos. Act 2 starts a minute into the performance. All in all, this is a gorgeous capture of old and new cast members... I'm not finished yet in this bootleg business but if I was, this could be my magnum opus.

collection.Notes: Some head and railing obstructions throughout the show. Misses the far right side of the stage but some nice close-ups and excellent sound on the video. First couple minutes of each act are blacked out, and there are a few quick dropouts throughout the show. 2 disc. Sunsetblvd79s master is better than this copy, this one is just for completionists.

collection.Cast: Eden Espinosa (Elphaba), Kate Reinders (Glinda), Derrick Williams (Fiyero), David Garrison (The Wizard), Carol Kane (Madame Morrible), Jenna Leigh Green (Nessarose), Logan Lipton (Boq), Steven Skybell (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes: Final Broadway Performance for Eden Espinosa, one disc or two disc version available

collection.Cast: Shoshana Bean (Elphaba), Megan Hilty (Glinda), David Ayers (Fiyero), Ben Vereen (The Wizard), Adinah Alexander (u/s Madame Morrible), Megan Sikora (u/s Nessarose), Jeffrey Kuhn (Boq), Sean McCourt (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes: About five minutes worth of cover-ups at the beginning (you see Glinda's entrance) because of latecomers and a few more random ones during the first Act. The first few minutes of the second Act are similar to those of Act One. No obstructions, and incredible close-ups. There are 1-disk and 2-disk versions of this video, so check before trading.

collection.Cast: Jessica Vosk (Elphaba), Ginna Claire Mason (Glinda), Jon Robert Hall (Fiyero), Tom McGowan (The Wizard), Isabel Keating (Madame Morrible), Catherine Charlebois (Nessarose), Sam Seferian (Boq), Harry Bouvy (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes: A wonderful capture of Jessica's final performance, with no obstructions or washout. Jessica gave a top-notch performance and added new riffs. There are a few quick dropouts during applause and a nice mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. Audio is perfect and curtain call is included. *Commonly mislabelled, the MP4 is Tornado With Pigtails' master from the same performance.

collection.Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Helen Dallimore (Glinda), Adam Garcia (Fiyero), Nigel Planer (The Wizard), Miriam Margolyes (Madame Morrible), Katie Rowley Jones (Nessarose), James Gillan (Boq), Martin Ball (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes: Great performance from Idina at the Halloween performance. Shot around a head, but worked around as well as possible. Has a few glitchy spots and missing the last three minutes. Some great vocals and riffs from Idina, not previously captured on video. B+

collection.Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Kristin Chenoweth (Glinda), Norbert Leo Butz (Fiyero), Sean McCourt (u/s The Wizard), Carole Shelley (Madame Morrible), Michelle Federer (Nessarose), Randy Harrison (t/r Boq), William Youmans (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes:
This is Kristin and Norbert's last show. It was also supposed to be Joel Grey's last show, but he was ill. Video is sound only for the first 4 minutes and for the last 30 seconds of Defying Gravity. This is a great video with excellent sound. Includes the speech by Idina. This bootleg is a treasure.
Multiple copies are circulating. The best is the 7.8 GB file, which has higher video resolution, working menus, and the playbill. The 7.63 GB file has the same video quality but no playbill, skips 1 minute in late Act 1 (A Sentimental Man (Reprise)), skips the first 1.5 minutes of Act 2, and the smalls may glitch such that the last section of Act 1 doesn't play at all. The 4.07 GB file has worse video quality but does have a working menu, and includes the playbill and various extras (TV spots, Macy's special, Seth's Broadway Chatterbox).

collection.Cast: Emmy Raver-Lampman (s/b Elphaba), Gina Beck (Glinda), Timothy A. Fitz-Gerald (u/s Fiyero), Kyle Brown (u/s Fiyero), Tim Kazurinsky (The Wizard), Alison Fraser (Madame Morrible), Brenda Hamilton (u/s Nessarose), Jesse JP Johnson (Boq), Tom Flynn (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes: A perfect capture of the current First National Tour with some understudies. Emmy is a phenomenal Elphaba with great acting choices and amazing riffs. Very interesting to see a Fiyero mid-show switch; Kyle jumps in to Act Two seamlessly. One of my best captures with no blackouts, no washout, no obstruction, and very steady filming. This is shot slightly wider than usual, particularly when the ensemble is on stage, so you can see a lot more of the action. There are still close-ups when necessary. As usual, it's filmed in 16:9, and the sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.
collection.Venue: SAFE Credit Union Performing Arts Center

collection.Cast: Emmy Raver-Lampman (s/b Elphaba), Gina Beck (Glinda), Nick Adams (Fiyero), Tim Kazurinsky (The Wizard), Alison Fraser (Madame Morrible), Jenny Florkowski (Nessarose), Jesse JP Johnson (Boq), Tom Flynn (Doctor Dillamond)
collection.Notes: Beautiful HD capture with no obstructions and clear sound; Emmy has an amazing voice.

collection.Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Helen Dallimore (Glinda), Adam Garcia (Fiyero), Nigel Planer (The Wizard), Miriam Margolyes (Madame Morrible), Katie Rowley Jones (Nessarose), James Gillan (Boq), Martin Ball (Doctor Dillamond)

Beautiful capture with amazing closeups. Performances were fantastic all the way around. Teal is wonderful and Megan is at the top of her game. A+

Cantwell (Nessarose), Zachary Noah Piser (Boq), Michael Genet (Doctor Dillamond), Christopher King (Chistery), Sterling Masters (Witch's Mother), William Ryall (Witch's Father/Ozian Official)
collection.Notes: Great performances from both Rachel and Ginna. Nicely captured with no obstruction, just a couple quick dropouts and some occasional washout. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

collection.Cast: Megan Hilty (Performer), Julia Murney (Performer)
collection.Notes: This was supposed to be a Kristin Chenoweth concert but due to an illness, these two stepped in, which made for some great jokes about Kristin. The two have great chemistry and it's their first time performing together. It was really a sensational concert with great stories and wonderful vocals that must be heard. Songs include: All That Jazz, Back to Before, Ring Them Bells, Till There Was You, I Could Have Danced All Night, Part of Your World, The Wizard and I, Popular, Defying Gravity, For Good, and I Will Never Leave You. A+

wants.Notes: Major technical difficulty right after Tattletale and the show stopped for about 15 minutes. When it resumed, Allison Janney ad-libbed to Andy Karl, I'm sorry I'm so late, but the elevator broke down. Andy totally lost it and it took him a few seconds of stumbling through his lines to stop laughing. It was hysterical. Also, SJB, Allison and Megan were VERY goofy during the pot-smoking scene and all kinds of things were going on—Allison was squirting whipped cream into her mouth from the can, Megan choked a bit when she took a puff of the cigarette, and Stephanie found a Cheeto on the floor while she was pretending to crack up and went Oh! A Cheeto! and popped it in her mouth. Also, apparently the guy next to master had allergies or something as he kept sniffling through all of Act 1.

wants.Notes: Lots interesting changes -- the end of Out of Control (no more SJB high note), new written-in line for SJB during the pot-smoking scene (which just about made me die of laughter), some re-worked lines for Joe, parts of Well-Oiled Machine now in Mundania, and lots of other little things. SJB sounded sick (evident in Dance O Death). Also have to add that this audience—while much smaller due to it being a weekday—was INSANE. Very loud laughers, but they had a great energy, I suppose.

wants.Notes: This is the performance where the set broke before the show and they couldn't get the main lift in the center of stage to work at all, so they did the show without it. This meant no bed, desks, copy machine, pot scene living room set, car…etc.  Also, it was Michael X. Martin's second time on as Mr. Hart, so there were a few minor slip-ups. And because of all the insanity, the cast did a lot of ad-libbing.

wants.Notes: This show is magical, Phillipa is captivating, and the entire ensemble is perfectly quirky. There have been a few changes since Berkeley that tighten up the show a bit and make it ready for Broadway. A near perfect capture with no washout, no obstruction, and just one very quick dropout. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

This is nearly perfectly captured with no obstruction and just a couple quick very dropouts. It’s filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes playbill scans.

wants.Notes: Very good capture of Nat as Barbara! Some shakiness throughout but not too bad. Some washout is seen throughout but nothing enough to take away from the video. Nat is absolutely fantastic as Barbara and she proves to be the absolute best and most underrated cast member. Also has Will Blum as Otho, but only as Otho. Eric Anthony Johnson plays all the ensemble parts Will should be playing for the Otho track. This causes some issues in songs with big ensemble parts such as Beautiful Sound and the Reprise where a clone is missing. Overall a fantastic capture of a new Barbara u/s. 1080p.

wants.Notes: Excellent 4K capture of Natalie as Delia. There’s a very small obstruction in Act One that only blocks a little bit of action on the very front and right of the stage. Although there are a lot of tight closeups in order to avoid washout, the action is followed very well. There is occasional wandering, unfocusing, and one very brief partial blackout near the end of Act One. Includes curtain call, audio is fed from external source.

wants.Cast: Alex Brightman (Beetlejuice), Dana Steingold (u/s Lydia Deetz), Kate Bailey (u/s Barbara Maitland), David Josefsberg (Adam Maitland), Adam Dannheisser (Charles Deetz), Natalie Charle Ellis (u/s Delia Deetz), Michelle Aravena (Miss Argentina), Kelvin Moon Loh (Otho), Danny Rutigliano (Maxie Dean), Brooke Leigh Engen (u/s Maxine Dean / u/s Juno), Nevada Riley (u/s Girl Scout), Ryan Breslin (Ensemble), Elliott Mattox (Ensemble), Mateo Melendez (Ensemble), Commodore C. Primous III (Ensemble), Graham Stevens (Ensemble), Michael Bryan Wang (Ensemble), Katie Lombardo (s/w Ensemble), Eric Anthony Johnson (s/w Ensemble)
wants.Notes: First full video of Dana, Kate, and Nevada as Lydia, Barbara, and The Girl Scout respectively. Up until Ready Set is blind-shot due to major usher activity, leading to all of Invisible not being captured besides the ceiling. Lydia's last line in Invisible before Alex's entrance in TWBDT is when you actually start seeing the stage. After this usher leaves, the video is an almost perfect capture of the show. Some washout is seen but it's very minimal and mostly in wide shots. Includes curtain call. Edited by the AMAZING bikinibottomday. Dana's third Lydia performance of the Remount. Dana was absolutely amazing and did some great vocal riffs! Alex was once again on his game. Kate was so good as Barbara, she has that Kerry Essence. Once again Nat kills it as Delia, proving to be my fav. Brooke's Juno and Maxine was hilarious and Nevada crushed it as the Girl Scout.

wants.Notes: Filmed in 4K from loge, released in 1080p and 4K. Closing performance. Mid-show Lancelot switch when Jordan Donica left prior to the tournament near the end of Act 1, and understudy Christian Mark Gibbs took over for the rest of the show. Occasional brief obstruction from the guy diagonally in front of me who kept leaning forward to peek at his playbill, usually only when an actor goes downstage and stage right. Otherwise no other obstructions, and dropouts only for applause. Slightly grainy at times due to maxing out on zoom. Includes curtain call.

wants.Master Notes: Good 4K capture of Emma Pittman’s very limited run as Roxie. There were several moments of blackouts, wandering, etc as ushers were active, primarily in scene transitions. There is also some minor obstruction in the form of a head at the bottom of the stage, and occasionally the master’s covering. Spotlight washout is fairly frequent but rarely obscures a character’s face. Includes curtain call and most of the entr’acte, audio is fed from external source.

Minor obstruction from a couple heads, but shot around as best as possible. A few brief drop-outs. Great getting to see Nicholas Rodriguez as David and Jacob Dickey as Paul.

wants.Notes: Different night from the March 2022 (1) video, during the week Jennifer Simard was on as Joanne. Filmed on phone from front mezzanine, cropped and fine-tuned in post editing, mostly wide stage-shot with minimal zooms, external audio source. Act 1 looks far better than Act 2 (unsure if Act 2 was just too dark or if phone settings were off). Act 1 is fairly clear and well-captured. Act 2 is dark and grainy, and drifts from action a couple of times. See screenshots for overview. Never to be sold except by master, never to be posted anywhere online.

wants.Notes: Filmed in 4K, released in 1080p and 4K, from almost dead center mezzanine. A head can be seen in some shots, but almost never obstructs the actors aside from maybe their legs here and there. Difficult to shoot some scenes steadily due to need to stretch as tall as possible to see over the head. An electric closing performance, several lengthy standing ovations throughout the show. Includes video of curtain call and speeches at the end. Never to be sold except by master, never to be posted in part or in whole anywhere online.

wants.Master Notes: Fresh off of opening night and what a night it was. The audience was lively and eating it up. This show is absolutely hilarious and goes deeper than the movie, and both Megan and Jen are belting their faces off into the rafters. Poor Michelle can't act nor can she walk in her costumes, but god can she sing. Full original broadway cast here as well. Recorded from the second row center balcony. The Lunts mix always gets funky in the balcony so it's not the clearest in regards to the hearing the full orchestrations and lyrics, and sometimes both ladies are just wailing and you loose lyrics. Megan forgot her lyrics in Tell Me, Ernest and literally just started mumbling but got back on pretty fast. Jen and Chris held it together the best they could but everyone onstage was laughing. Can't wait to go back.

wants.Master Notes: Notes: An excellent quality audio taken from the right orchestra. You may hear some shuffling at the end of Act 1 as I was trying to get a bit more comfortable and the phone kept trying to slip off. Natalie was just absolutely incredible as Helen and truly is a MUST SEE. Her chemistry with Megan is wonderful as well. The audience absolutely loved the show and gives great applause. I believe this is the first audio with Kyle Brown as Stefan.

wants.Notes: Would love to see lists, though I typically don't mind just sharing.

wants.Master Notes: Recording of a performance with quite an enthusiastic audience! A nice capture of Justin O'Brien in the show on his Broadway debut day (this recording captures his second ever performance in DBH). There is quite a bit of overmodulation throughout, which does unfortunately affect the quality of the recording, but it is still listenable and enjoyable. All in all, a wonderful show!

wants.Master Notes: Great capture of this immensely hyped production! Shot in a great mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups; the far right side of the stage is often cut off by a bar or lighting fixtures, but it's worked around well and not much action is missed because of this. Minimal wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. Audio is patched in from an external source. Includes bows. Slightly Zegler-focused; can you blame me? Overall a great capture of this inevitably Broadway-bound summer blockbuster.

wants.Master Notes:
If you enjoy Hadestown but also don't feel the need to be able to see it, then this is the video for you.

The exposure was set at essentially zero, and the obstructions are incredibly annoying, so this video functionally is a game of spotting the cast, similar to 2019 movie 'cats' which is almost a game of celebrity spotting, but I digress, this was filmed reasonably far back so the occasional glimpses of the cast are quite small.

Video is act 2 only, Includes audio of act 1.

wants.Cast: Javier Muñoz (Alexander Hamilton), Lexi Lawson (Eliza Hamilton), Sydney James Harcourt (u/s Aaron Burr), Renée Elise Goldsberry (Angelica Schuyler), Christopher Jackson (George Washington), Andrew Chappelle (u/s Marquis de Lafayette / u/s Thomas Jefferson), Ephraim Sykes (u/s Hercules Mulligan / u/s James Madison), Anthony Ramos (John Laurens / Philip Hamilton), Jasmine Cephas Jones (Peggy Schuyler / Maria Reynolds), Rory O'Malley (King George III), Roddy Kennedy (s/w Philip Schuyler / s/w James Reynolds / s/w Doctor), Thayne Jasperson (Samuel Seabury), Neil Haskell (Charles Lee), David Guzman (s/w George Eacker)
wants.Notes: More new performers captured in this phenomenal show. Lexi is lovely as Eliza and makes the role her own. Standout understudies were Sydney and Andrew—both give fantastic performances as Burr and Lafayette/Jefferson. My best capture of this show to date. It's fully captured with no washouts and no blackouts, just a couple very quick dropouts between scenes. There are a couple heads that can be seen occasionally, but they are worked around and don't obstruct the action. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

wants.Cast: Javier Muñoz (alt Alexander Hamilton), Phillipa Soo (Eliza Hamilton), Leslie Odom Jr. (Aaron Burr), Renée Elise Goldsberry (Angelica Schuyler), Nicholas Christopher (s/b George Washington), Daveed Diggs (Marquis de Lafayette/Thomas Jefferson), Okieriete Onaodowan (Hercules Mulligan/James Madison), Andrew Chappelle (u/s John Laurens/u/s Philip Hamilton), Alysha Deslorieux (s/b Peggy Schuyler/s/b Maria Reynolds), Rory O'Malley (King George III), Sydney James Harcourt (Philip Schuyler/James Reynolds/Doctor), Thayne Jasperson (Samuel Seabury), Daniel J. Watts (s/w George Eacker)
wants.Notes:
Javier is fantastic in the lead role; he's very different from Lin—a bit darker, more intense, and less care-free, but just as passionate. The understudies are great as well, but Andrew is the stand-out; his nuanced performance as Philip is heartbreaking. Seeing the show with so many new faces proves that it has legs beyond the original cast and will continue to be a hit. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.
This is the correct date for the low quality video labeled as June 16, 2016.

wants.Notes: Excellent HD capture of the Angelica Tour cast with David Hyun-Soo Park during his first week as replacement Marquis de Lafayette/Thomas Jefferson! It's filmed in 16:9 with a mix of wides, mediums, and closeups from the rear left orchestra. Features fabulous performances from everyone in the cast despite the two-show day, with great reactions from the audience. There are some rare minor obstructions at the very bottom of the screen during wideshots and there are quick dropouts during applause. A few seconds after Room Where it Happens is blacked out but audio is included. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

wants.Cast: Deejay Young (s/b Alexander Hamilton), Cherry Torres (s/b Eliza Hamilton), Josh Tower (Aaron Burr), Stephanie Umoh (Angelica Schuyler), Paul Oakley Stovall (George Washington), David Park (Marquis de Lafayette/Thomas Jefferson), Tyler Belo (Hercules Mulligan/James Madison), Jon Viktor Corpuz (John Laurens/Philip Hamilton), Olivia Puckett (Peggy Schuyler/Maria Reynolds), Peter Matthew Smith (King George III), Connor Wince (s/w James Reynolds/Philip Schuyler/Doctor), Patrick Garr (Samuel Seabury), Robbie Nicholson (Charles Lee), Taylor N Daniels (George Eacker), Lisa Finegold (s/w The Bullet), Natalie Kaye Clater (Ensemble), Phil Colgan (s/w Ensemble), Lencia Kebede (Ensemble), Paige Krumbach (Ensemble), Krystal Mackie (Ensemble), Avery Sobczak (s/w Ensemble)
wants.Master Notes: HD capture of Deejay Young as Alexander and Cherry Torres post-lockdown debut as Eliza. It's filmed in 16:9 with a mix of wides, mediums, and closeups; Act One is filmed partially blind from the rear left orchestra around a lot of heads and is mostly wides or closeups, and Act Two is filmed closer forward and to the left and features more closeups as well as a mix of wides and mediums. These seats weren't optimal for filming at all and there's a large amount of obstructions, wandering, and dropouts through the entire show, but the performances were fabulous and the audio picks it up very well. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

wants.Cast: Edred Utomi (Alexander Hamilton), Zoe Jensen (Eliza Hamilton), Josh Tower (Aaron Burr), Stephanie Umoh (Angelica Schuyler), Paul Oakley Stovall (George Washington), David Park (Marquis de Lafayette / Thomas Jefferson), Tyler Belo (Hercules Mulligan / James Madison), Jon Viktor Corpuz (John Laurens / Philip Hamilton), Lencia Kebede (u/s Peggy Schuyler / u/s Maria Reynolds), Peter Matthew Smith (King George III), Connor Wince (s/w Philip Schuyler / s/w James Reynolds / s/w Doctor), Patrick Garr (Samuel Seabury), Desmond Nunn (s/w Charles Lee), Joshua Rivera (s/w George Eacker), Meghan Faddis (s/w Ensemble), Phil Colgan (s/w Ensemble), John Devereaux (Ensemble), Paige Krumbach (Ensemble), Krystal Mackie (Ensemble), Alaina Vi Maderal (s/w Ensemble), Kendyl Sayuri Yokoyama (s/w Ensemble)
wants.Notes: Excellent HD capture of Lencia Kebede's post-lockdown debut as Peggy Schuyler/Maria Reynolds! It's filmed in 16:9 with a mix of wides, mediums, and closeups from the mid-right mezzanine. Unfortunately, the first twenty minutes of the show are audio-only and the footage picks up at You'll Be Back. The first six minutes of Act Two are also audio-only. There are a few other blackouts: part of Meet Me Inside, Take a Break, and Burn are audio-only as audience members were moving around. There are very few obstructions: the very far right side of the stage is partially covered by a head but it usually never covers anything important and the stage-left balcony is blocked during Schuyler Defeated. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

wants.Notes: Excellent HD capture of one of Cherry Torres' first shows back as Peggy Schuyler/Maria Reynolds! It's filmed in 16:9 with a mix of wides, mediums, and closeups from the left mezzanine. The opening number is audio-only as the footage picks up at Aaron Burr, Sir; Act Two is almost complete with some quick dropouts early in the act due to latecomers. Includes two blackouts: the first lasts a minute during Nonstop due to a latecomer and Best of Wives and Best of Women is audio only. There are very few obstructions: the very bottom right side of the stage is partially covered by a head but it never covers anything important and the stage-left balcony is blocked throughout. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

wants.Notes:
Stunning HD capture of the Angelica Tour with understudies for Lafayette/Jefferson, Laurens, and Peggy/Maria—my best video of the show yet. It's filmed in 16:9 with a mix of wides, mediums, and incredible closeups from a left box. Deejey's hilarious performance as Jefferson was the standout of the performance, alongside Lencia's perfect Maria Reynolds. There are some extremely rare obstructions at the very bottom left of the screen during extreme wideshots but it's almost unnoticeable and there are a couple short dropouts for applause. Due to technical issues, Stay Alive is audio-only and the video picks back up during Ten Duel Commandments. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.
***Please do not post any part of the video, download link, or any of the screencaps online, such as YouTube / Tumblr / Facebook / Discord / Reddit / Instagram / TikTok.***

wants.Notes: Beautiful HD video of the new Broadway cast of Hamilton! There are a few drop-outs and obstructions here and there in Act One but overall it's worked around. Act One is mostly close-ups and starts at Laurens' rap. Act Two picks up in the middle of What Did I Miss? and features both close-ups and wider shots. I did move during intermission so Act Two has almost no obstructions, but honestly I don't even care. This performance—this cast—deserved to be captured on video. The colors pick up gorgeously, my favorite parts of the staging are captured just right, and I even rested my camera on my backpack to help steady it when I was overcome with emotion and shaking. Just wow.

wants.Master Notes: A near perfect capture and the first known DVD of this shows Off-Broadway run. Barrett is incredibly talented: amazing vocals, acting and comedic timing. Dan and Charissa are both fantastic in their roles as well. The show is absolutely hysterical, much improved from the movie version. The first minute of Act One is blacked out, but the show is otherwise fully intact with no blackouts and only slight washout in a few wide shots. There is no obstruction, the video is steady, the sound is excellent, and its captured very well with the usual mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. Its filmed in 16:9 and includes curtain call, encore, and playbill scans.
wants.Notes: Available as both a widely circulated DVD, and a much rarer 1080p Blu-Ray.

wants.Notes: Not so great wide-shot phone boot from the front of the Mezz. Mostly unobstructed. One or two tiny scenes cut off by the speakers/tech on the right side of the stage. Unfortunately, a lot of focusing issues throughout. Not great for a first-time watcher of the show, but nice to see some of the projections/choreography/staging. This show was a couple days before opening night and the cast was all on their A game, and the audience was levitating. Thoroughly enjoyed the show and can't wait to go back and can't wait til other video masters get recordings of it. One of the best theater experiences I've had in years.

wants.Notes: Super fun seeing this show with Robin de Jesús as Usnavi! Plus a number of understudies for this performance. Steady, clear 1080p HD 16:9 widescreen capture with minimal obstruction, only a couple of heads on the farthest downstage and sides of the stage. Mix of wides, medium, and close zooms to capture individual performances and ensemble numbers (the choreography was fun!). First minute or so of each act is audio only due to ushers, very few dropouts otherwise. Audio of a fundraising speech at the end of the show. Audio fed in from external source so it's super clear.

wants.Notes: Second Preview. A scenery mishap in the first act in the scene before I Know Things Now causes the show to be paused, and James Lapine makes a brief announcement from the stage. Features a number of songs and scenes that would eventually be cut or rewritten, especially in Act 2. This includes the cut songs Boom, Crunch! (in place of Last Midnight), Have to Give Her Someone, The Plan, and the full version of Second Midnight. The filmer follows the action excellently, with only one brief hide/extended period of wandering during The Plan.

wants.Notes: Filmed in 4K, released in 1080p and 4K. Debut performance for Sebastian, Stephanie, Montego, Krysta, Andy, and Katy in their respective roles. Act 1 filmed using alternative method, so sometimes a little slow to follow or frame action, tended towards wider shots in some scenes; obstruction when action was at the very front of the stage. Switched to regular method after first song of Act 2 when people who'd moved made the former method too obstructed; much less obstruction for the rest of Act 2. Enthusiastic audience gave lots of cheers for the new cast members, drowning out some dialogue/lyrics at times. Never to be sold except by master, never to be posted anywhere online.

wants.Notes: A few seconds from the beginning of Act 1 and Act 2 are audio only. Those few seconds from Act 2 are of the young childs doll sitting centre stage, crying, just before they pick it up. Occasionally a head on the left side of the screen is in view. 10 minutes in Act 1 had to be used from a recording from another date due to a theatre related issue causing the show to stop. In that footage there is one brief drop out and heads in frame in the wide shots.I have tried my best to incorporate this footage as seamlessly as possible. The image can sometimes be dark due to the lighting. Filmed mainly in wides and mediums. Recording includes the curtain call and a shot of the outside of the theatre.

wants.Notes: Proshot. Aired on March 15, 1991, but in reality it was filmed between May 23 – 25, 1989. Filmed as a part of the Season 10 premiere episode of PBSs American Playhouse. Almost the entire original Broadway cast (the only exception being Snow White, who was pregnant) returned to film it.

wants.Master Notes: The annual Hollywood Bowl production never disappoints. Jennifer absolutely kills it, and there are some brilliant comedic moments from the whole cast. This capture is nearly perfect with just a couple quick dropouts and some brief moments where the video wanders or goes out of focus, but never for more than a few seconds. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call, encores, and playbill scans.

Some wandering during Telly as the house lights were on.

wants.Cast: Tori Feinstein (Matilda), Christopher Sieber (Miss Trunchbull), Alison Luff (Miss Honey), Lesli Margherita (Mrs. Wormwood), Matt Harrington (Mr. Wormwood), Alex Brightman (Michael Wormwood), Celia Mei Rubin (Mrs. Phelps), Phillip Spaeth (Rudolpho), Mitchell Sink (Bruce), Grace Capeless (Lavender), Marcus D'Angelo (alt Nigel), Beatrice Tulchin (Amanda), Ted Wilson (alt Eric), Analise Scarpaci (alt Alice), Ava DeMary (Hortensia), Chris Sumpter (Tommy), Jennifer Bowles (Acrobat), Ben Thompson (Escapologist), Matt Meigs (Doctor), Sean Montgomery (Entertainer / Sergei)
wants.Master Notes: An excellent capture of Tori as Matilda. She's a very impressive young actress and has her own fresh take on the role. The rest of the cast is solid as well; I believe this is the first capture of Alison Luff, and she steps into the show beautifully. This is very nicely captured with no washout, no major blackouts, and very little obstruction. A railing blocks the very front left corner of the stage, but only the actors' legs are unseen. Telly is audio only; the video for Act Two begins when the house lights go down and Lavender takes the stage. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans. 2 discs.
wants.Notes: There are a couple of versions going around that do not match the above description. One version is a VOB+smalls where Act 2 is just a duplicate of Act 1. There is another with a different actress playing Miss Honey. Before trading, make sure to check and verify that you are sharing the correct version. In the correct set of files, the Act 2 menu says Act 1, but the video is otherwise right and matches the master notes.

wants.Notes: Good 4K capture of this cast! The filming is quite rough at points, usually too wideshot or poorly framed. Some head obstruction on both sides, though primarily on the left, that blocks some action but is typically worked around well. Increased wandering/readjustment and unfocusing throughout. About ten minutes from the end of Act One, there was a show stop, and the rest of the act afterwards has more obstruction due to latecomers. Includes curtain call, audio is fed from external source.

NO PART OF THIS VIDEO IS EVER TO BE POSTED/SHARED/GIFTED ANYWHERE ONLINE including but not limited to YouTube, Tumblr, Vimeo, Reddit, Torrents, social media, etc. Never to be sold except by master.

wants.Master Notes: Excellent capture of this incredible show! Welcome to the Moulin Rouge is messy and somewhat obstructed but the capture cleans up and the obstruction is worked around well after that. Much of the action on the catwalk is only seen from the torso up. Shot in a gorgeous mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. Some increased wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. A few dropouts due to usher activity and latecomers. Audio is patched in from an external source, some rustling and scratching can be heard throughout. Some spotlight washout can be seen. Overall a wonderful capture of this stunning show with two of the most perfect leads I’ve ever seen.
