Includes curtain call, and encore, audio is fed from external source.

wants.Master Notes: Video properly starts at Time. The person in front of me could not pick a direction to lean their head, and every time they moved it took a few seconds to readjust (most noticeable in ltlo and my days). Sea turtle scene is completely obstructed, as well as any action on the far left side of the stage (“I played piano once”). The end of the show is wideshot and video cuts out before the final note due to a standing ovation. Between the wandering and obstruction this isn’t a video for someone unfamiliar with the show, since a lot of action is missed.

Not all the action is missed during this time, but the majority is. Great audio throughout, however.

wants.Master Notes:
Filmed in 4K, released as 1080p and 4K. Virtually no obstructions after a bit of settling in, excellent filming  throughout. Super fun getting to see Salisha on as Snow. Dropouts mainly only for applause and late-comers. Mix of wide shots and closer zooms for individual performers. Noisy talkative literal toddlers behind ue for Act 1, moved for Act 2 so they are less audible. 
NFT (NOT FOR TRADE) and sales only through master until May 28, 2025.

wants.Notes: The first ~13 minutes are heavily obstructed, and sometimes fully dark, due to an unfortunate head blocking most of the action in front of the kitchen set piece, but luckily the person moved and every other musical number is mostly unobstructed and steady, with the action followed well. Scenes are a little more shaky, but besides working around the head at times and some minor drops throughout, it is consistent and clear! Most main action, especially in the center, is captured and big numbers like SUTBM are great. Both acts are shot start to finish. Ciara and Gerianne are both fantastic stepping into their respective roles!

wants.Notes: Filmed in 4K, also available as 1080p HD. First full musical filmed with the new camera! External audio source. Absolutely beautiful picture (further enhanced in post editing), very steady, following the action extremely well (perks of knowing the show well!). A little washout and a couple heads can be seen in the widest shots (but never really get in the way of anything). Mostly mid-range zoom, a few close-ups. Sara Bareilles was on fire (rewarded with a 45-second applause after SUTBM), and the audience was very enthusiastic. Funny little blooper/ad-lib when Caitlin dropped her tray during Christopher's second poem.

There is quite a bit of wandering – and the flying part of Defying Gravity was shot fully blindly – but as a whole I'm glad this video came out the way it did.

wants.Master Notes:
Exciting night for Lencia's Broadway debut as the first full-time Black Elphaba, Jenna's debut as Wicked's first wheelchair-using performer, NaTasha's Wicked debut, Allie's and Daniel's principal debuts. Excellent audio from the mezzanine. Lots of cheering at expected parts of the show. A little shuffling during I'm Not That Girl when the older couple nearby decided to gather their things and leave. Zip file includes both tracked and untracked .mp3 files.
Please do not post/gift/freely share this audio or any clips anywhere, including but not limited to YouTube, Tumblr, Instagram, Reddit, Vimeo, TikTok, or any other places online. This audio is never to be sold.

wants.Master Notes:
Filmed in 4K from mezzanine. Excellent capture, very steady filming. Virtually no obstructions, only a few dropouts, one of which was when the older couple neaby decided to gather their things to leave during I'm Not That Girl. Exciting night for Lencia's Broadway debut as the first full-time Black Elphaba, Jenna's debut as Wicked's first wheelchair-using performer, NaTasha's Wicked debut, Allie's and Daniel's principal debuts. Lots of cheering at expected parts of the show. Includes curtain call. 
NFT (NOT FOR TRADE) and sales only through master until November 28, 2025.

wants.Notes: Composer's Worktape. Available on archive.org. This demo consists of Stephen Schwartz singing and explaining cut songs and earlier versions of songs in the final show. Includes 8 tracks: Bad Situation, Bad Situation (Continued), Making Good, Making Good (Earlier Version), Step By Step, We Deserve Each Other, What Is This Feeling (Earlier Version), and Which Way Is The Party.

wants.Master Notes: Recorded from the front row of the dress circle. Clear audio. Includes a playbill shot, photos of the cast board and understudy board. Also includes a video of the curtain call. There was a show stop at the start of the scene where Elphaba and Glinda meet the Wizard, about 5 mins later there was an announcement that the show was continuing (included in the audio). Due to this act 1 is in 2 files. I started having some vocal tics towards the end of act 2, they shouldn't be audible, but just incase you hear any random humming, those are my tics. Please gift upon request.

wants.Master Notes: Excellent capture of the amazing new (ish) touring cast of Wicked! Shot with alternate camera in an excellent mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. Some wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout, mostly during applause breaks and scene transitions. Overall a truly great capture of this wonderful cast.

wants.Cast: Alexia Khadime (Elphaba), Christine Tucker (s/b Glinda), Ryan Reid (Fiyero), Michael Fenton Stevens (The Wizard), Sophie-Louise Dann (Madame Morrible), Caitlin Anderson (Nessarose), Joshua Clemetson (u/s Boq), Graham Kent (Doctor Dillamond), Conor Ashman (Chistery)
wants.Master Notes: Great capture of this stunning show. Some minor obstruction by a small safety rail and heads at the bottom that don't take away much, if at all, from the action. A couple of small dropouts. Shot in a fantastic mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. The show's lighting truly was loving me with this capture. Some spotlight washout can be seen on wider shots. Some increased wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout and especially in act 2. Overall a great video.

wants.Cast: Victoria Matlock (Elphaba), Christina DeCicco (Glinda), Cliffton Hall (Fiyero), P.J. Benjamin (The Wizard), Barbara Tirrell (Madame Morrible), Deedee Magno Hall (Nessarose), Josh Lamon (Boq), Tom Flynn (Doctor Dillamond), Leslie Becker (Midwife), Lori Holmes (Witch's Mother), Paul Slade Smith (Witch's Father / Ozian Official)
wants.Notes: Beautiful capture and no obstructions. This features the optional high note by Glinda in the opening, which is a thrill. The performances were amazing, especially Glinda, who was at her best on this day. The cast seemed to have extra energy and gave a tad more than usual. A+. Sometimes the location is mislabeled as Hippodrome Theatre, Baltimore, Maryland because the playbill scanned at the beginning is wrong.
wants.Venue: David A. Straz Jr. Center for the Performing Arts

There are occasional moments of blurriness, but the quality and general steadiness makes up for that. It's filmed in 16:9, with a mix of wides, mediums and close-ups. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

Throughout the show, she looks at the camera and basically performs for it. Examples are I'm Not That Girl and Defying Gravity. She notices it pretty much straight away at the beginning of Wizard and I.
wants.Notes: T/W: This video contains a PETA advertisement at the beginning, which contains graphic images and may be distressing.  This was included by the master on release.

This was supposed to be Jackie Burns’ last night, but she’d been sick since late January and ended up missing this performance. Donna is just INCREDIBLE as Elphaba… Master has released a new 2023 remaster that is available through him.

wants.Notes: Great capture of JV Elphaba! Starts during NOMTW, right before Glinda enters. Shot with two different cameras from the same angle, so the picture quality occasionally varies slightly. My HD camera ran out of battery during A Sentimental Man, so I switched right to my 720p camera for the rest of Act One and all of Act Two, minor shakiness and occasional washout, but overall the video quality is excellent.

wants.Notes: What a show, high energy from everybody! Vocally Eden stuggled a lot in The Wizard and I, but she picked it up by the end of the first act. Megan also had some vocal trouble in the beginning, but also picked it up toward the end, she also flubbed... she said nudified instead of moodified. By the end of act two, everyone onstage was in tears! The audience was crazzzzyyyyy, but not as crazy as I had thought they would be. Fantastic evening.

wants.Notes: Fantastic HD capture of the tour's electric Reopening Night. It's filmed in 16:9 with a mix of wides, mediums, and closeups from the rear left orchestra. In addition to the occasion of the tour's reopening, this is also the first full-length video released of the full 2020 cast and the first full tour video filmed from the orchestra in years. Talia is a fabulous Elphie and brings a powerful take to the role alongside Allison's perfectly hilarious performance as Glinda. There are no real obstructions: some heads appear at the bottom of the screen during wides, though it doesn't affect more than the actors' legs for the most part and dropouts are mostly limited to applause breaks and early in each Act during latecomer seating. One annoying latecomer can be slightly heard talking throughout the show (even during songs). The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

wants.Notes:
Caissie & Kris final performance. Recently re-transferred and re-edited, 2 DVDs. Shot in 16:9 widescreen from very back and side of mezzanine, max zoom, so a little shaky and some washout in wider shots. Shot around two heads in front as best as possible. A couple minutes total of blackouts due to tape degradation. Caissie went ALL OUT for her final performance, doing some riffs that she normally didnt do (especially Defying Gravity and OMG her Fiyero riff in No Good Deed!) and was fierce as all heck. LOTS of cheering from a loving and appreciative audience (and whistling from Caissies then-boyfriend), wanting to send Caissie and Kris off with all the love possible. Includes video of Megans emotional farewell speech for them.
NOT FOR TRADE (NFT) FOREVER

I may have been loving Caissies acting so much that I fully stopped following Megan for a bit in “Popular” and just focused on Caissie, because Caissie was making THE BEST FACES. Adam Lambert (before he shot to pop-star fame) was such a great Fiyero (one of my favorites) and what a lovely voice!

wants.Notes: Includes pre-show announcement and curtain calls. Also includes the final changes carried over from London, including the abbreviated Wonderful and the pretty light show replacing the big black hat. Shot from rear mezz, no obstructions. One minute coverup after the ballroom (before Popular) and another during Thank Goodness. Several mic problems cause actors voices to cut out, which causes hilarity when the Wizards head mouths silently in Act Two. Really painful Act One for Julia, especially the end of WAI which is half screamed/sung. She apparently got the frog out of her throat during intermission, because she comes back for a triumphant Act Two. Overall, Julia makes some lovely acting choices and has brilliant chemistry with the rest of the cast, especially Katie and Sebastian; their ALAYM is spell-binding. Will never be a fan of her voice, though. Katie is even better than she was in November. The real standout was Cristys Nessarose... she has the best voice and gives the most compelling performance.

wants.Notes:
Recorded before the show had ever been staged. Some minor plot differences and some different lyrics, and includes some songs that never made it to the show we know and love today (example: As If By Magic, part of a plot line that followed closer to the original novel.) No orchestra - the instrumental is just a piano backing everything. Stage directions spoken (example going into ALAYM: Scene four, a cheap rented room).
Recently replaced with a new, clearer, copy—this one including somewhere around 30 tracks, compared to 12.

wants.Notes: Nice capture of the Second National Tour cast, with a big mishap when the power suddenly goes out in the middle of Thank Goodness. Helene and the rest of the cast keep right on going until a crew member comes onstage and stops the show. The DVD includes video of the entire debacle until the curtain goes down, and it continues for a bit after this, then cuts out, then resumes again when the show is picked back up during Wicked Witch of the East. As for the video, the first part of the show is mostly blacked out, but once it gets to The Wizard and I, it's much better. The dragon's head blocked Marcie's face during Defying Gravity, but other than that, there's virtually no obstruction. Overall, an amazing capture with no spotlight washout, amazing colour and sound.

wants.Cast: Julia Murney (Performer), Stephanie J. Block (Performer)
wants.Notes:
Conducted by Steven Reineke. Essentially the same concert as The Wicked Divas concert that Ms. Block and Ms. Murney had done together prior to this concert, just renamed for this Boston Pops gig.
1) All That Jazz - SJB & Murney, 2) Dont Rain on My Parade - SJB, 3) Back to Before - Murney, 4) Think of Me - SJB, 5) Ring Them Bells - Murney, 6) No More Tears - SJB & Murney, 7) My Heart Will Go On - SJB, 8) Divas Lament - Murney, 9) Popular - Murney, 10) Defying Gravity - SJB, 11) For Good - SJB & Murney, 12) I Will Never Leave You - SJB & Murney

wants.Cast: Julia Murney (Performer), Stephanie J. Block (Performer)
wants.Notes: Recorded at an outdoor venue and there was some noise from wind and so on. A thunderstorm caused a mass exodus of people running for cover during Defying Gravity and was the reason the concert ended early.
